_comment: User learning preferences for the study-agent. Edit this file to customize your study experience.
_max_items_note: Maximum number of new items added to the study queue per nightly curation run. Prevents the queue from growing faster than you can review it.
_language_note: Language for generated concept summaries and self-check questions. Options: English, Korean, Japanese, etc. The agent will produce output in this language regardless of source language.
_skip_topics_note: Topics matching these strings (case-insensitive substring) will never be added to the study queue. Use this to filter out routine operational tasks that are not worth studying.
_priority_topics_note: Topics matching these strings are automatically elevated to 'high' priority in the study queue. Use this to ensure critical knowledge areas get reviewed first.
_candidate_signals_note: Keywords that indicate a study candidate when found in an Airlock record. Extend this list to match your own workflow patterns.
_output_format_note: Format for study queue files: 'json' (structured, machine-readable) or 'markdown' (human-readable, suitable for Obsidian).

deduplication._window_note: Look back this many days when checking for duplicate topics. Items added within this window for the same topic are skipped.
deduplication._similarity_note: How to compare topics for deduplication. 'substring' = simple text match. 'semantic' = use RAG similarity (requires RAG to be configured).

review_session._show_source_note: When showing review items, include the first 300 characters of the original Airlock record for context.
review_session._require_explicit_note: If true, operator must explicitly mark each item as known/failed/skip. If false, no input means 'skip'.

notifications._send_note: Send a completion message to cron-agent via sessions_send after each run. cron-agent then routes to the appropriate channel.
notifications._item_list_note: If true, the completion message includes the list of new items. If false, just the summary counts. Keep false for large queues to avoid message spam.
